concept: Amend / Formally Change Part of a Continuing Matter
description: To formally change part of something still living, a rule, a decision, an institutional arrangement, while keeping its history and identity intact. The exact old and new wording belongs in a source record beside the Phi.
sound_symbolism: Light 'he' begins with the same moving breath heard in 'helui' (change). At the stressed middle, 'la' lays the tongue into broad 'a' and opens the word wide. Crisp 'ki' makes one exact contact at the end. The whole contour breathes, spreads across the open center, and leaves a clear final mark.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the amending agent as subject and the continuing matter as object: 'ha kowela ha nasholu helaki.' (The council amends this rule.) Preserve exact changed wording, version, date, authority, and effective conditions in a separate source record. The event-noun rule supplies amendment. Distinct from 'helui' (change), correction, replacement, repeal, implementation, and review.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Amendment treats institutional forms as conditioned and revisable while preserving the causal and ethical history of earlier versions.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Named amendment prevents authorities from presenting a changed rule as timeless or quietly erasing the wording under which people previously acted.

tags.activity: formally changing part of a continuing rule or record
